sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good progress! Clicking ring at (20, 42) spawned a new blob at (38, 33). The blob moved:
(38,33) → (47,28) → (51,26) → (53,25) and got stuck
G went from 12 → 9 pixels. The blob got stuck at (53, 25) with G at 9 pixels.
Now I have:
- Blob stuck at (53, 25) - can't click it (game over)
- G has 9 pixels remaining
- I need to click G pixels to reduce them further
Let me check where G pixels are and click them. The pattern has been:
1. Move blob toward G (blob shrinks G when adjacent)
2. Click G pixels to reduce further
3. G stabilizes at some minimum
Let me try clicking the G pixels now. But I need to be careful not to click trail pixels.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blob stuck at (53, 25), G has 9 px. Click G pixels to reduce.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
m_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'M']

print(f"M: {[n['boundary'] for n in m_nodes]}")
for g in g_nodes:
    print(f"G: px={g['pixels']}, b={g['boundary']}")

# Get all G pixels
all_g = []
for g in g_nodes:
    all_g.extend(g['boundary'])
print(f"All G: {all_g}")

# Filter to only G pixels near the cluster (bottom area, row >= 55)
g_cluster = [p for p in all_g if p[0] >= 55]
print(f"G cluster (row>=55): {g_cluster}")

# Click G cluster pixels
for p in g_cluster:
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': p[0], 'col': p[1]}])
    
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    nodes = seg['nodes']
    g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
    g_px = sum(n['pixels'] for n in g_nodes) if g_nodes else 0
    
    print(f"After {p}: G_px={g_px}")
    if g_px == 0:
        print("G eliminated!")
        break
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
        break
    if last_action_result.get('game_over'):
        print("GAME OVER!")
        break

# Check remaining G
if g_nodes:
    remaining = []
    for g in g_nodes:
        remaining.extend(g['boundary'])
    print(f"Remaining G: {remaining}")
    
    # Check if remaining are trail pixels (row < 55)
    trail_g = [p for p in remaining if p[0] < 55]
    cluster_g = [p for p in remaining if p[0] >= 55]
    print(f"Trail G: {trail_g}, Cluster G: {cluster_g}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
